rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and avoid package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference visible features or declared helpers; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type contracts.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use valid AADL scalar types: int, real, bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with proper syntax and scoping.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current AADL scope or declared in the same annex.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or use subcomponent ports in type contracts.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst AADL structural extraction details
